title: EVA EXTRA extracted files and interviews
display_title: EVA EXTRA extracted files and interviews
wikitext:
[]: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by blue » Sun Mar 01, 2020 7:17 pm Hey there! I managed to extract the recently released interviews from the EVA-EXTRA app! As part of khara's new premium subscription service, 14 of 25 interviews from the 1.0 CRC have been released on the app. The recently released softcover reissues of the CRCs (1.0 and 2.0) don't include any interviews, which khara conveniently mentions as a reason to publish (some of) them digitally. Apparently khara also plans to release interviews from the 2.0 CRC and promises to publish some exclusive content (maybe interviews) regarding the upcoming 3.0+1.0 movie on the app in the near future. The interviews are released as raw text on the app, which makes it very easy to copy, paste and translate! I wanted to make this accessible to the community in order to make future translations of these interviews easier. I plan to update this post as new content gets added to the EVA-EXTRA app in the future! All the interviews are in html format. They can be opened with any browser. Should work on phones as well. For the images to appear, the interviews need to stay in the same folder as their corresponding images. The interviews are numbered as originally released in the 1.0 CRC. Enjoy! edit (March 2nd, 2020): Joseki noted that there's a mistake in the digital version of interview 06: 松原 is mistakenly noted as 樋口 in the second part of the interview. He fixed it in his CRC thread! I'd like to note that I will not change this in the html files, as I want to keep everything accurate to the EVA-EXTRA app. I fixed and updated the file for interview 06. If you happen to find any mistakes in the digital interviews when comparing it to the print version, please message me about it! edit (March 9th, 2020): I added a link to an archive with all the files I've extracted from the EVA-EXTRA app so far! The password is evageeks . edit (April 24th, 2020): I updated the archive with the new 3.0+1.0 logo. A few new images were added here and there. I added a link to the new audio commentary for 1.11. The password is evageeks . edit (April 25th, 2020): I added links to the new audio commentaries for 2.22 and 3.33. The password is evageeks . edit (May 13th, 2020): All the links are dead for now. edit (September 20th, 2020): Nine more interviews were added to the app! As of now, 23 of 25 interviews from the 1.0 CRC have been released digitally through EVA-EXTRA. edit (October 16th, 2020): Accompanying the release of the third trailer for 3.0+1.0, a new flyer is being distributed in movie theatres across Japan. This flyer was also added to the EVA-EXTRA app.

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by kuro hazama » Fri Apr 24, 2020 12:04 pm Do you know any way to rip the streams in the app?, Ive been trying to rip the audio commentary for the first film these last couple of days and I dont know where to start.

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by blue » Fri Apr 24, 2020 3:56 pm kuro hazama wrote: Do you know any way to rip the streams in the app?, Ive been trying to rip the audio commentary for the first film these last couple of days and I dont know where to start.

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by intermediateO » Sun Apr 26, 2020 12:52 pm I watched all the audio commentaries, and while most of it was all just normal chit-chat, there were a few interesting bits. Nothing too juicy though, cause whenever there were explicit Shin Eva spoilers, they would cut to audio of Soul's Refrain instead. 1.0: Nothing really noteworthy to me. 2.0: When Asuka first meets Shinji, sliding down Unit 02, Megumi Ogata comments on how it's nice to see the old, normal Asuka and Rei. When the shot then cuts to Toji and Kensuke, she comments on getting to see them as they are normally too, and then cuts herself off, saying she probably shouldn't say anything more. (Pretty much confirming time skip Toji and Kensuke.) Some of the staff say that 2.0 was originally much more similar to 1.0, being much more adherent to the plot of the TV series, but after 1.0's success and seeing that not just old otaku, but large groups of middle schoolers were also coming to see the new Eva, Tsurumaki told them they should "break" Eva. This is what led to getting a 2.0 that breaks away from the TV series as it goes on. Ogata chimes in though that she always felt the new movies were going to be very different ever since she read the scene in 1.0 where Misato goes down to Central Dogma with Shinji. During the fight with Zeruel, almost for 5 full minutes, Megumi Ogata gets replaced with Soul's Refrain because she's talking about Shin Eva and it's final scene and what she thinks Eva is really about. Once the song finally stops, she comes back and asks the viewers to please come back and watch this part again once they've seen Shin Eva. She thinks they'll understand her feelings. (It is ambiguous if she means watching the Zeruel fight, or the commentary itself, which has not been confirmed at all to be released uncensored upon the release of Shin Eva or anything yet.) They call the preview at the end "problematic". 3.0: That original shutter silhouette stunt at one of the Eva stores that first revealed the character designs for 3.0 was originally gonna have more characters, but then they realized it was too spoilery and cut some. (Again, probably Toji, Kensuke, and Hikari.) A staff member comments on how surprised they were to see the Key of Nebuchadnezzar hadn't appeared again yet by 3.0. He's then surprised again when they say he won't get Soul's Refrain'd for his comment since he stated it vaguely enough. They mentioned that the main complaint they kept getting over and over after it premiered was that people didn't understand it, but since 3.0 and Shin were supposed to originally be billed together, they hope that people can expect most of those answers in the second half.

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by robersora » Sun Apr 26, 2020 3:10 pm oh wow, this is amazing, I was wondering what they were talking about. thank you so much for sharing!

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Apr 26, 2020 6:36 pm Yeah, this is pretty cool, thanks for documenting it!

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Apr 26, 2020 7:41 pm Excited nerd squee! Thank you! Those are all awesome to hear!

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by kuro hazama » Sun Apr 26, 2020 11:01 pm Wow, so good! Thank you for putting this up, I was going crazy trying to scrape data from the android app or coming up with something, would really like to know how you did it as Im still learning about apps and this kind of tinkering! BTW, I also watched the commentaries and the two last live watches they did yesterday with eva staff and Yoko Takahashi on youtube that basically were a second audio commentary to all effects, while I snatched the stream video of those last two, I couldnt see or get the video of the first one for 1.0, which was kind of chaotic apparently and plent yof people complained on how badly organized it was, on the other side, Yoko Takahashi showed up with a Unit 01 costume so Its a pity I didnt get to see it, I can upload the live watches If thats alright whenever I find somewhere to upload it since my gdrive and Mega are pretty full. Dunno If I should share my impression on all commentaries here or in the the spoiler section because they got juicy enough to get some vague guesses about the last film´s contents, some are soft confirmations of things in the film, while some other are things that are very likely to be on it considering that the staff were commenting on it, like one of the staff trying to remember if the interior of the Key of nabuchednezzar was a spoiler or if it already showed up before, while talking about it in a way that hints of what it is when the special guest tried to guess it(If true, Its one of the theories people have discussed here), the case is that there was a lot of spoilers in the conversation, which is the reason they obscured them playing Tamashii no rufuran during those segments, If someone tried listening and wondered what that was about. There was of course production stuff all around and I suppose Its okay to say that finally we got confirmation that right now the complete records for Q are being worked on. Also, Does somebody know about a character or thing called Rover/Rober/Roba/Roobaa?, because Im got the impression we may just have learned the name of our new eva mascot unless I understand they are talking about something else(and I can get confused easily!)

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 26, 2020 11:17 pm kuro hazama : Yeah, whenever you have an opportunity go ahead. Such things might get lost if the community doesn't save them.

Once the song finally stops, she comes back and asks the viewers to please come back and watch this part again once they've seen Shin Eva. She thinks they'll understand her feelings.

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by kuro hazama » Mon Apr 27, 2020 8:41 am I did found the first live watch link but the video was set to private so If anyone by chance captured the stream that would be great to upload. I think Ill post some impressions in the spoiler section.

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by kuro hazama » Mon Apr 27, 2020 8:46 am Oh Rover right. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: EVA-EXTRA: extracted files & interviews             Quote Post by intermediateO » Mon Apr 27, 2020 9:31 am Oh! You're right! Roba (ろば/驢馬) is donkey! I actually do remember looking it up now and getting the right definition. I probably forgot because it was the last thing of 2.0 and then I went right to watching 3.0. Hmm... I do still remember one word I just couldn't place though... Oh well. If I ever go back and watch the commentaries again, hopefully it'll come up again.
